name: server-management
description: Server management principles and decision-making. Process management, monitoring strategy, and scaling decisions. Teaches thinking, not commands.
capabilities: Guides process management tool selection (PM2, systemd, Docker, Kubernetes)
Teaches monitoring strategy and key metrics prioritization
Explains log management principles and rotation strategies
Provides scaling decision frameworks for different scenarios
Outlines security principles for server access and updates
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged two instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3 and 166. After manual review, these are FALSE POSITIVES. Line 3 contains only a description string in YAML frontmatter, and line 166 contains only instructional text. The file contains no cryptographic code, no network requests, no file system operations, no environment access, and no external command execution. This is purely educational content teaching server management concepts.
valueStatement: Learn server management thinking and decision-making for production systems. This skill teaches process management, monitoring strategy, and scaling principles rather than memorized commands.
limitations: Does not execute any server commands directly
Does not provide specific configuration files
Does not include installation or setup instructions
Focuses on principles rather than implementation code

useCases.title: DevOps Learning
useCases.description: New developers learning server operations and production concepts

useCases.title: Architecture Decision Making
useCases.description: Engineers making decisions about monitoring and scaling strategies

useCases.title: System Troubleshooting

promptTemplates.title: Process Management Basics
promptTemplates.prompt: What process manager should I use for a Node.js application in production?

promptTemplates.title: Monitoring Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: What key metrics should I monitor for a web application?
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up monitoring for a new service

promptTemplates.title: Scaling Decisions
promptTemplates.prompt: When should I scale horizontally versus vertically?

promptTemplates.title: Health Check Design
promptTemplates.prompt: What should a proper health check include?

bestPractices: Start monitoring from day one rather than adding it later
Use structured logging (JSON format) for easier parsing and analysis
Implement auto-restart configurations to handle crashes automatically
Choose health check depth based on load balancer requirements
antiPatterns: Running services as root instead of non-root users
Ignoring logs or skipping log rotation setup
Manual restarts instead of configuring auto-restart
Skipping backups or not having a regular backup schedule

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands on my server?
faq.answer: No. This skill teaches principles and decision-making frameworks. It does not run any commands or modify any files.

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance for tools like PM2, systemd, Docker, Kubernetes, Grafana, and others mentioned in the principles.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains concepts in plain language with decision tables. It is designed for learners who want to understand thinking, not memorize commands.

faq.question: Does this skill include configuration examples?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on principles and decision criteria. It does not provide specific configuration files or code snippets.

faq.question: How is this skill different from server documentation?
faq.answer: Documentation tells you what commands to run. This skill teaches why to choose certain approaches and when to use them.

faq.question: Can I use this for troubleshooting production issues?
faq.answer: Yes. The troubleshooting priority section provides a systematic approach: check process, logs, resources, network, then dependencies.
